mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized household demand growth that drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization that boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months where INDPRO increases, or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns specifically during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 236.0 triggers inflation expectations that force discount rate repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 236.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness that suppresses equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The negative interaction signal produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the correlation between the two series breaks down without affecting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real GDP growth directly expand corporate revenue baselines, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 changes, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below 50 percent across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 104.0 during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints that compress profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of months where INDPRO drops below 104.0 during tightening periods, or the regime-specific effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
